destinations.evidence: The enum discriminants and TryFrom mapping decode 7/8/9 as ImageCall/AudioCall/VideoCall and classify them as executor capabilities.
Registry reads decode the stored capability byte and retain services whose decoded capability is an executor capability.

equivalence_argument: The capability byte is decoded from registry storage before filtering through is_executor_capability, so image/audio/video registry records are retained as executor services.
notes: The reader tests also assert try_from(7/8/9) and executor classification for the media capabilities.
rationale: The diff adds ImageCall, AudioCall, and VideoCall enum variants with discriminants 7, 8, and 9, updates TryFrom<u8>, marks them in is_executor_capability, and adds tests for those mappings. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: The TEE registry reader must decode capability values 7, 8, and 9 as ImageCall, AudioCall, and VideoCall respectively, and treat all three as executor capabilities.
suggested_falsification_tests: Assert Capability::try_from(7/8/9) returns ImageCall/AudioCall/VideoCall and that is_executor_capability is true for each, while 10 still returns Err. Then read a mocked registry service record containing each capability and assert it is retained as an executor service.
summary: Image, audio, and video capability values are decoded and retained.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Assert Capability::try_from(7/8/9) returns ImageCall/AudioCall/VideoCall and that is_executor_capability is true for each, while 10 still returns Err. Then read a mocked registry service record containing each capability and assert it is retained as an executor service.
title: Image audio video are executor capabilities
